name: The Jabberwock
primary_sources: Through the Looking-Glass by Lewis Carroll

distinctive_features: leathery wings similar to a pterodactyl
long scaly neck and tail resembling a sauropod
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: bipedal with wings and a long tail

domain_specific_visual.Victorian_influence: reflects Victorian fascination with natural history and paleontology

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: represents the unknown and the absurd in a dreamscape

relationships.description: The Jabberwock is slain by the hero in the poem.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the whimsical and nonsensical elements of Carroll's work
primary_sources: Through the Looking-Glass by Lewis Carroll
